mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5%, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and boosting consumer demand, which lifts S&P 500 earnings expectations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue visibility and driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive moves in HOUST and PAYEMS fail to produce positive SPX 60-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output, which historically leads equity markets by one week as supply chain bottlenecks ease and profit margins expand.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume reflects weakening consumer spending, which directly compresses forward earnings estimates for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances following a >5% RSAFS drop.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, real interest rates turn negative, triggering inflation hedging flows out of equities into commodities and bonds, depressing S&P 500 returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPI and sub-0.5% funds rate fails to predict negative SPX 60-day returns in >50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate of 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand, which mechanically supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% fails to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 jobs signals a sudden labor market acceleration, which rapidly improves near-term consumer confidence and drives immediate positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll beats of >150k do not lead to positive SPX 5-day returns in >60% of instances.
